equations.label: eq:movers
equations.description: Movers model: child outcome $Y_i$ decomposed into origin $o(i)$ and destination $d(i)$ effects.

qa.question: What does psi_d identify in the movers model?
qa.answer: The place effect of destination CZ d

equations.label: eq:exposure-pf
equations.description: Exposure effects regression: child outcome on destination place effect, conditional on origin.

equations.label: eq:place-fe
equations.description: Place fixed effect: demeaned mean outcome for permanent resident children in CZ $c$.

equations.label: eq:decomp-place
equations.description: Variance decomposition of child outcomes into origin, destination, and residual components.

equations.label: eq:childhood-gradient
equations.description: Childhood exposure gradient: exposure effect varies by age at move $a$.

equations.label: eq:selection
equations.latex: D_{oc} = \Pr(\text{move to } c \mid \text{origin} = o) = f\!\left(\dot{\psi}_c - \dot{\psi}_o,\; x_{i,t-1}^{(k)},\; \ddot{X}_{o}\right)
equations.description: Selection equation: probability of moving from $o$ to $c$ depends on place effect differential.

equations.label: eq:ch-estimator
equations.description: Chetty-Hendren estimator: IV estimator projecting residualized outcomes on place effects.

equations.label: eq:forecast-bias
equations.description: Forecast-bias correction: shrinkage correction for estimation error in place effects.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy?
qa.answer: Comparing siblings who moved at different ages to the same destination, exploiting variation in childhood exposure duration

qa.question: What is the main finding on childhood exposure?
qa.answer: Each additional year of exposure to a one-rank-better destination raises earnings rank by 0.024 ranks

qa.question: How does the exposure effect vary with age at move?
qa.answer: It declines from 0.452 for moves at age 0-5 to 0.118 for moves at age 16-19
